audit_items.proof_trace: All pointer dereferences in ldap_sync.c are guarded: every potentially-NULL return value (ctrls, ber, syncUUIDs) is checked before use via 'if (ptr == NULL) goto done' patterns. The one gap—calling ber_init(retdata) at L465 without explicitly checking retdata for NULL—is handled safely in production builds because ber_init itself checks 'if (bv == NULL) return NULL', and the result is immediately checked by 'if (ber == NULL) goto done' at L466-468. No actual null pointer is dereferenced in ldap_sync.c under any reachable path in release builds.
